verses.reason: Opening line of the Book of Mormon, establishes Nephi's gratitude and parentage.
Establishes the theme: God's tender mercies toward the faithful.
Nephi's testimony of gaining his own witness through crying unto the Lord.
The foundational promise: inasmuch as ye keep my commandments, ye shall prosper.
The most quoted verse in the Book of Mormon — 'I will go and do.'
'Led by the Spirit, not knowing beforehand' — a model for spiritual guidance.
'It is better that one man should perish than that a nation should dwindle in unbelief.'
Nephi's stated intent: to persuade men to come unto the God of Abraham.
Description of the Tree of Life whose fruit is 'most sweet, above all.'
'The course of the Lord is one eternal round' — a key doctrinal statement.
'He that diligently seeketh shall find' — promise of revelation.
'I know that he loveth his children; nevertheless, I do not know the meaning of all things.'
The Tree of Life is identified as the love of God.
Promise of blessing to those who seek to bring forth Zion.
Summary of the Lamb's doctrine: all men must come unto Him to be saved.
'There are save two churches only' — the Church of the Lamb and the church of the devil.
The word of God (rod of iron) can overcome the fiery darts of the adversary.
'By small means the Lord can bring about great things' — the Liahona lesson.
'If God had commanded, He would provide the means' — faith principle.
Nephi rebukes his brothers: 'He hath spoken unto you in a still small voice.'
Prophecy of the Messiah yielding Himself to be smitten and scourged.
'Can a woman forget her sucking child? Yea, they may forget, yet will I not forget thee.'
'Ye need not suppose that the righteous are lost' — closing assurance.
'Awake, my sons; put on the armor of righteousness' — Lehi's powerful exhortation.
'There must needs be an opposition in all things' — foundational LDS philosophy.
'Adam fell that men might be; and men are, that they might have joy.'
Prophecy of the choice seer named Joseph, a clear reference to Joseph Smith.
'O wretched man that I am!' — the iconic opening of the Psalm of Nephi.
'I will not put my trust in the arm of flesh' — Nephi's resolve to trust God.
'We lived after the manner of happiness' — a concise summary of Nephite aspiration.
'An infinite atonement' — Jacob's defining phrase for Christ's sacrifice.
'When they are learned they think they are wise' — warning against intellectual pride.
'To be spiritually-minded is life eternal' — a key doctrinal principle.
'Cheer up your hearts... ye are free to act for yourselves' — agency and optimism.
'Reconcile yourselves to the will of God, and not to the will of the devil.'
'All things which have been given of God... are the typifying of him' — Christ in all things.
'A virgin shall conceive, and shall bear a son, and shall call his name Immanuel.'
'His name shall be called Wonderful, Counsellor, The mighty God, The everlasting Father, The Prince of Peace.'
'The wolf also shall dwell with the lamb' — iconic Millennial imagery.
'The Lord JEHOVAH is my strength and my song; he also has become my salvation.'
'How art thou fallen from heaven, O Lucifer, son of the morning!'
'By grace that we are saved, after all we can do' — most debated BoM verse on grace.
'We talk of Christ, we rejoice in Christ, we preach of Christ.'
'He inviteth them all to come unto him... black and white, bond and free.'
'I will proceed to do a marvelous work among this people.'
'The devil cheateth their souls, and leadeth them away carefully down to hell.'
'A Bible! A Bible! We have got a Bible' — the Lord's rebuke of scripture-rejecters.
'Press forward with a steadfastness in Christ... feasting upon the word of Christ, and endure to the end.'
'Feast upon the words of Christ; for the words of Christ will tell you all things what ye should do.'
'If ye believe not in these words, believe in Christ' — Nephi's final appeal.
The voice of Christ to Nephi: 'On this night shall the sign be given.'
Mormon's personal declaration: 'I am a disciple of Jesus Christ.'
'I am Jesus Christ the Son of God. I created the heavens and the earth.'
'Offer for a sacrifice unto me a broken heart and a contrite spirit.'
Christ's lament: 'How oft would I have gathered you as a hen gathereth her chickens.'
God the Father introduces Christ: 'Behold my Beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.'
'I am the light and the life of the world; I have drunk out of that bitter cup.'
The Father, Son, and Holy Ghost are one — foundational Godhead statement.
'Be perfect even as I, or your Father who is in heaven is perfect.' — with 'I' added post-resurrection.
'Seek ye first the kingdom of God and his righteousness.'
'Ask, and it shall be given unto you; seek, and ye shall find.'
'I am the law, and the light. Look unto me, and endure to the end.'
'Have ye any that are sick? Bring them hither and I will heal them.'
Christ blesses the children and angels minister to them in encircling fire.
The coming forth of the Book of Mormon as the sign of the gathering.
'My kindness shall not depart from thee, neither shall the covenant of my peace be removed.'
'Search these things diligently; for great are the words of Isaiah.'
'Bring ye all the tithes... and prove me now herewith' — the tithing promise.
'I will send you Elijah the prophet before the coming of the great and dreadful day.'
'He shall turn the heart of the fathers to the children' — foundation of temple work.
'If it be called in my name then it is my church' — naming the Church.
'What manner of men ought ye to be? Even as I am.'
The people are all converted and there are no contentions among them.
They had all things common — no rich or poor, bond or free.
No envyings, strifes, or tumults — a description of Zion society.
There could not be a happier people among all who had been created by God.
No robbers, murderers, Lamanites, or any manner of -ites — total unity.
'Come unto Christ, and be perfected in him' — Amaleki's powerful invitation.
'Offer your whole souls as an offering unto him' — the ultimate call to consecration.
'I will tell you of the wrestle which I had before God' — the iconic opening of Enos's prayer experience.
'My soul hungered; and I kneeled down before my Maker' — the model of sincere, desperate prayer.
'Thy sins are forgiven thee, and thou shalt be blessed' — the Lord's direct response to Enos.
'I shall see his face with pleasure' — Enos's testimony of the joy of redemption.
Mormon's declaration of faith: 'The Lord knoweth all things which are to come; wherefore, he worketh in me to do according to his will.'
King Benjamin labored 'with all the might of his body and the faculty of his whole soul' — model of consecrated leadership.
Jacob magnifies his office to teach 'with all diligence' — model of priesthood responsibility.
'Think of your brethren like unto yourselves' — foundational teaching on equality and charity.
'Before ye seek for riches, seek ye for the kingdom of God.'
'Look unto God with firmness of mind, and pray unto him with exceeding faith' — comfort to the pure in heart.
'We search the prophets... and our faith becometh unshaken' — power of prophetic study.
'They looked beyond the mark' — the Jews' rejection of Christ's plainness.
'What could I have done more for my vineyard?' — the Lord's lament over His people.
'Cleave unto God as he cleaveth unto you' — the reciprocal nature of divine love.
'I truly had seen angels, and they had ministered unto me' — Jacob's personal witness against Sherem.
The Lord's promise: 'I will go before thee into a land which is choice above all the lands of the earth.'
'This is a land which is choice above all other lands' — the covenant promise for the Americas.
'My Spirit will not always strive with man' — warning against neglecting God.
'Never has man come before me with such exceeding faith' — the Lord's declaration about the brother of Jared.
Christ reveals His pre-mortal spirit body: 'I am he who was prepared from the foundation of the world.'
'Whatsoever thing persuadeth men to do good is of me' — a key test for discerning truth.
They 'did sing praises unto the Lord' while being tossed by the sea — faith in adversity.
Moroni's warning: nations that uphold secret combinations 'shall be destroyed.'
'Suffer not that these murderous combinations shall get above you' — call to vigilance.
'Whoso believeth in God might with surety hope for a better world' — the connection between faith and hope.
'Faith is things which are hoped for and not seen' — the Book of Mormon's definition of faith.
'I give unto men weakness that they may be humble... my grace is sufficient' — the most quoted verse in Ether.
Prophecy of the New Jerusalem to be built upon the Americas.
'The prophets and the priests did prick their hearts with the word, continually stirring them up unto repentance.'
The Nephites prospered 'inasmuch as they kept the commandments' — the conditional promise.
'In the end of this book ye shall see that this Gadianton did prove the overthrow of the Nephite people.'
'The sanctification cometh because of their yielding their hearts unto God.'
'It is upon the rock of our Redeemer... that ye must build your foundation' — the most quoted verse in Helaman.
'Thou hast not sought thine own life, but hast sought my will' — the Lord's commendation of Nephi.
'All things shall be done unto thee according to thy word' — the sealing power granted.
'Except the Lord doth chasten his people... they will not remember him.' — Mormon's central thesis.
'Ye have sought for happiness in doing iniquity, which thing is contrary to the nature of that righteousness which is in our great and Eternal Head.'
'Ye are free; ye are permitted to act for yourselves' — a key statement on agency.
'I did endeavor to preach... but my mouth was shut' — Mormon forbidden to preach to the rebellious.
'Their sorrowing was not unto repentance... but it was rather the sorrowing of the damned.' — defining false repentance.
'I did utterly refuse from this time forth to be a commander' — Mormon's moral stand.
'O ye fair ones, how could ye have departed from the ways of the Lord!' — Mormon's lament.
'O that ye had repented before this great destruction had come upon you.'
'Know ye that ye must come to the knowledge of your fathers, and repent of all your sins and iniquities.'
'Behold, I speak unto you as if ye were present, and yet ye are not' — Moroni sees our day.
'Why do ye adorn yourselves with that which hath no life, and yet suffer the hungry... to pass by you?'
'He is the same yesterday, today, and forever' — the unchangeable nature of God.
'Whoso believeth in Christ, doubting nothing, whatsoever he shall ask the Father... it shall be granted.'
I will not deny the Christ.
The sacramental prayer on the bread — used verbatim in the modern LDS church.
The sacramental prayer on the wine/water — used verbatim in the modern LDS church.
The Spirit of Christ is given to every man, that he may know good from evil.
Charity suffereth long, and is kind — parallel to 1 Corinthians 13.
Charity is the pure love of Christ, and it endureth forever.
Little children are whole, for they are not capable of committing sin.
Little children are alive in Christ.
My son, be faithful in Christ; and may the grace of God rest upon you forever.
Ask God with a sincere heart and real faith — Moroni's promise.
By the power of the Holy Ghost ye may know the truth of all things.
Come unto Christ, and be perfected in him — the final invitation.
'When ye are in the service of your fellow beings ye are only in the service of your God' — one of the most quoted BoM verses.
'There shall be no other name given nor any other way... whereby salvation can come unto the children of men.'
'The natural man is an enemy to God... unless he yields to the enticings of the Holy Spirit.'
'Believe in God; believe that he is, and that he created all things.'
'We have no more disposition to do evil, but to do good continually.'
'A seer can know of things which are past, and also of things which are to come.'
'God himself shall come down among the children of men' — Abinadi's Christological statement.
'He is the light and the life of the world; yea, a light that is endless, that can never be darkened.'
'Bear one another's burdens, that they may be light' — the baptismal covenant.
'Stand as witnesses of God at all times and in all things, and in all places.'
'I will also ease the burdens which are put upon your shoulders' — the Lord's promise in bondage.
'All mankind must be born again... changed from their carnal and fallen state.'
'They could not bear that any human soul should perish' — the missionary motivation.
'Have ye spiritually been born of God? Have ye received his image in your countenances?'
'He shall go forth, suffering pains and afflictions and temptations of every kind' — Christ succoring His people.
'Called and prepared from the foundation of the world according to the foreknowledge of God' — foreordination.
'I will give away all my sins to know thee' — Lamoni's father's prayer of total surrender.
'I know that I am nothing; as to my strength I am weak; therefore I will boast of my God.'
'O that I were an angel, and could have the wish of mine heart' — Alma's missionary desire.
'Faith is not to have a perfect knowledge of things; therefore if ye have faith ye hope for things which are not seen, which are true.'
'It must be an infinite and eternal sacrifice' — Amulek on the Atonement.
'I could remember my pains no more; yea, I was harrowed up by the memory of my sins no more' — Alma's conversion.
'By small and simple things are great things brought to pass.'
'Bridle all your passions, that ye may be filled with love.'
'These things are an abomination in the sight of the Lord; yea, most abominable above all sins save...'
'Wickedness never was happiness.'
'In memory of our God, our religion, and freedom, and our peace, our wives, and our children' — Title of Liberty.
'If all men had been like unto Moroni, the very powers of hell would have been shaken forever.'
'They had been taught by their mothers, that if they did not doubt, God would deliver them.'
'It mattereth not; I am not angry, but do rejoice in the greatness of your heart' — Pahoran's humble response.
'I sought for the blessings of the fathers' — Abraham's righteous desire for the priesthood.
The Abrahamic covenant: 'I will make of thee a great nation.'
'The intelligences that were organized before the world was; among all these there were many of the noble and great ones.'
'The Gods, organized and formed the heavens and the earth' — plural Godhead in creation.
'The Gods formed man from the dust of the ground, and took his spirit and put it into him.'
'I saw a pillar of light exactly over my head, above the brightness of the sun' — the First Vision.
'I must join none of them, for they were all wrong' — God's answer to Joseph's prayer.
'Upon you my fellow servants... I confer the Priesthood of Aaron' — John the Baptist restoring the Aaronic Priesthood.
A powerful promise that immersion in the scriptures provides protection against deception in the last days.
Commonly cited in LDS discourse as the key to navigating spiritual confusion.
The central admonition: be ye also ready, for in such an hour as ye think not, the Son of Man cometh.
'This is my work and my glory — to bring to pass the immortality and eternal life of man.' The most important verse in the PoGP.
'I, God, created man in mine own image' — foundational verse on man's divine origin.
Christ's submission: 'Father, thy will be done, and the glory be thine forever.'
Eve's declaration: 'Were it not for our transgression we never should have known good and evil, and the joy of our redemption.'
'All things bear record of me' — the symbolic nature of the physical world as witness of Christ.
'They were of one heart and one mind, and dwelt in righteousness; and there was no poor among them' — the definition of Zion.
'We believe in God, the Eternal Father, and in His Son, Jesus Christ, and in the Holy Ghost.' Foundational Godhead statement.
The definitive summary of the first principles and ordinances of the gospel.
The personal code of conduct: 'If there is anything virtuous, lovely, or of good report or praiseworthy, we seek after these things.'
'The truth endureth, and is always strong; it liveth and conquereth for evermore.'
'Blessed be the God of truth' — conclusion of Zerubbabel's winning speech.
'We will not hearken to the king's words, to go from our religion' — religious resistance.
'Victory standeth not in the multitude of an host; but strength cometh from heaven.'
'Every man sat under his vine and fig tree, and there was none to fray them.'

verses.text: How should thy vessel then be able to comprehend the way of the Highest?
verses.reason: A fundamental teaching on the inability of the finite mind to fully grasp the infinite.

verses.text: And the earth shall restore those that are asleep in her, and so shall the dust those that dwell in silence, and the secret places shall deliver those souls that were committed unto them.
verses.reason: A clear and poetic description of the resurrection.

verses.text: O thou Adam, what hast thou done? for though it was thou that sinned, thou art not fallen alone, but we all that come of thee.
verses.reason: A poignant lament on the universal effects of the Fall.

verses.text: Those are the ten tribes, which were carried away prisoners out of their own land in the time of Osea the king, whom Salmanasar the king of Assyria led away captive, and he carried them over the waters, and so came they into another land.
verses.reason: Foundational text for the LDS interest in the lost ten tribes.

verses.text: And it came to pass, when the forty days were fulfilled, that the Highest spake, saying, The first that thou hast written publish openly, that the worthy and unworthy may read it: But keep the seventy last, that thou mayest deliver them only to such as be wise among the people: For in them is the spring of understanding, the fountain of wisdom, and the stream of knowledge.
verses.reason: Supports the concept of sacred records reserved for the faithful.

verses.text: For it becometh not our age, said he, in any wise to dissemble, whereby many young persons might think that Eleazar, being fourscore years old and ten, were now gone to a strange religion.
verses.reason: A powerful statement on the importance of setting a righteous example for the youth.

verses.text: And when he was at the last gasp, he said, Thou like a fury takest us out of this present life, but the King of the world shall raise us up, who have died for his laws, unto everlasting life.
verses.reason: A definitive Intertestamental witness of the doctrine of the resurrection.

verses.text: For if he had not hoped that they that were slain should have risen again, it had been superfluous and vain to pray for the dead.
verses.reason: Explicit scriptural support for the connection between the resurrection and concern for the deceased.

verses.text: And my nation is this Israel, which cried to God, and were saved: for the Lord hath saved his people, and the Lord hath delivered us from all those evils, and God hath wrought signs and great wonders, which have not been done among the Gentiles.
verses.reason: Summarizes the miraculous nature of the Jews' survival.

verses.text: O Lord, Lord, the King Almighty: for the whole world is in thy power, and if thou hast appointed to save Israel, there is no man that can gainsay thee.
verses.reason: A powerful declaration of God's omnipotence and control over history.

verses.text: O my Lord, thou only art our King: help me, desolate woman, which have no helper but thee.
verses.reason: Expresses the core theme of total reliance upon God in times of extreme trial.

verses.text: And ye shall say, To the Lord our God belongeth righteousness, but unto us the confusion of faces, as it is come to pass this day, unto them of Juda, and to the inhabitants of Jerusalem.
verses.reason: A foundational verse for the book's theme of confession and divine justice.

verses.text: Afterward did he shew himself upon earth, and conversed with men.
verses.reason: Commonly cited as an early prophecy of the incarnation of Jesus Christ.

verses.text: For I sent you out with mourning and weeping: but God will give you to me again with joy and gladness for ever.
verses.reason: Captures the central promise of restoration for the exiled saints.

verses.text: Arise, O Jerusalem, and stand on high, and look about toward the east, and behold thy children gathered from the west unto the east by the word of the Holy One, rejoicing in the remembrance of God.
verses.reason: A quintessential description of the gathering of Israel.

verses.text: Then said Daniel, Because I may not worship idols made with hands, but the living God, who hath created the heaven and the earth, and hath sovereignty over all flesh.
verses.reason: A quintessential statement of faith distinguishing the living God from man-made objects.

verses.text: And Daniel said, Thou hast remembered me, O God: neither hast thou forsaken them that seek thee and love thee.
verses.reason: Expresses a fundamental doctrine of God's constant watchcare over His faithful servants.

verses.text: For ye cannot find the depth of the heart of man, neither can ye perceive the things that he thinketh: then how can ye search out God, that hath made all these things, and know his mind, or comprehend his purpose?
verses.reason: A powerful statement on the inscrutability of God's wisdom and the error of testing Him.

verses.text: For thy power standeth not in multitude, nor thy might in strong men: for thou art a God of the afflicted, an helper of the oppressed, an upholder of the weak, a protector of the forlorn, a saviour of them that are without hope.
verses.reason: A beautiful description of God's character as the champion of the vulnerable.

verses.text: Then said Ozias unto her, O daughter, blessed art thou of the most high God above all the women upon the earth; and blessed be the Lord God, which hath created the heavens and the earth, which hath directed thee to the cutting off of the head of the chief of our enemies.
verses.reason: A blessing that emphasizes Judith's unique role in the salvation of Israel.

verses.text: For thou art righteous in all the things that thou hast done to us: yea, true are all thy works, thy ways are right, and all thy judgments truth.
verses.reason: A profound acknowledgment of divine justice even in the midst of extreme trial.

verses.text: Like as in the burnt offerings of rams and bullocks, and like as in ten thousands of fat lambs: so let our sacrifice be in thy sight this day, and grant that we may wholly go after thee: for they shall not be confounded that put their trust in thee.
verses.reason: Reflects the doctrine that a broken heart and contrite spirit are the true sacrifices required by God.

verses.text: O all ye works of the Lord, bless ye the Lord: praise and exalt him above all for ever.
verses.reason: The opening refrain of one of the most famous hymns of praise in the Apocrypha.

verses.text: Thou, O Lord, according to thy great goodness hast promised repentance and forgiveness to them that have sinned against thee: and of thine infinite mercies hast appointed repentance unto sinners, that they may be saved.
verses.reason: A powerful declaration of the doctrine that repentance is a gift from a merciful God.

verses.text: And now I bow the knee of mine heart, beseeching thee of grace.
verses.reason: A beautiful and frequently quoted image of internal humility and submission to God's will.

verses.text: I humbly beseech thee, forgive me, O Lord, forgive me, and destroy me not with mine iniquities.
verses.reason: The core petition of the prayer, reflecting the universal human need for divine pardon.
Declares Christ as creator of all things visible and invisible, establishing his cosmic preeminence.
Reveals the mystery of the gospel: Christ in you, the hope of glory.
Classic warning against philosophy and vain deceit not grounded in Christ.
Buried with Christ in baptism and raised through faith — central Pauline baptismal theology.
Set your affection on things above — foundational spiritual orientation teaching.
Above all put on charity, the bond of perfectness.
Let your speech be always with grace, seasoned with salt — practical wisdom for Christian discourse.
Dispensation of the fulness of times gathering all things in Christ — key Restoration doctrine.
Sealed with the Holy Spirit of promise — foundational to LDS temple theology.
By grace are ye saved through faith — one of the most quoted verses on the doctrine of grace.
Built upon the foundation of apostles and prophets — cornerstone of LDS church organization.
To know the love of Christ which passeth knowledge — Paul's soaring prayer for spiritual comprehension.
Apostles, prophets, evangelists, pastors, teachers — the fivefold ministry that defines church organization.
No more tossed to and fro by every wind of doctrine — a call to mature, grounded faith.
Husbands love your wives as Christ loved the church — the model for covenant marriage.
A man shall leave father and mother and cleave to his wife — foundational marriage teaching.
Put on the whole armour of God — one of the most memorable images in all of scripture.
We wrestle not against flesh and blood — defines the true nature of spiritual conflict.
Even if an angel preaches another gospel, let him be accursed — a standard for doctrinal fidelity.
A man is not justified by the works of the law but by the faith of Jesus Christ — Paul's thesis statement.
I am crucified with Christ: nevertheless I live — one of the most personal and powerful verses in Paul's writings.
The law was our schoolmaster to bring us unto Christ — classic teaching on the purpose of the Mosaic law.
Neither Jew nor Greek, bond nor free, male nor female: all one in Christ — radical equality in the gospel.
No more a servant but a son, and heir of God through Christ — defines the believer's new identity.
Stand fast in the liberty wherewith Christ hath made us free — the battle cry of Christian freedom.
The fruit of the Spirit: love, joy, peace — the definitive list of Christlike attributes.
Whatsoever a man soweth, that shall he also reap — the law of the harvest, universally quoted.
Let us not be weary in well doing — enduring encouragement for perseverance.

verses.text: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
And the Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us... full of grace and truth.
Behold the Lamb of God, which taketh away the sin of the world.
This beginning of miracles did Jesus in Cana of Galilee, and manifested forth his glory.
Jesus answered and said unto them, Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up.
Jesus answered, Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born of water and of the Spirit, he cannot enter into the kingdom of God.
For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son...
But whosoever drinketh of the water that I shall give him shall never thirst.
God is a Spirit: and they that worship him must worship him in spirit and in truth.
Verily, verily, I say unto you, The hour is coming, and now is, when the dead shall hear the voice of the Son of God: and they that hear shall live.
Search the scriptures; for in them ye think ye have eternal life: and they are they which testify of me.
And Jesus said unto them, I am the bread of life: he that cometh to me shall never hunger.
Then Simon Peter answered him, Lord, to whom shall we go? thou hast the words of eternal life.
If any man will do his will, he shall know of the doctrine, whether it be of God, or whether I speak of myself.
Judge not according to the appearance, but judge righteous judgment.
Then spake Jesus again unto them, saying, I am the light of the world: he that followeth me shall not walk in darkness.
And ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall make you free.
Jesus said unto them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Before Abraham was, I am.
I must work the works of him that sent me, while it is day: the night cometh, when no man can work.
One thing I know, that, whereas I was blind, now I see.
I am the good shepherd: the good shepherd giveth his life for the sheep.
And other sheep I have, which are not of this fold: them also I must bring...
Jesus said unto her, I am the resurrection, and the life: he that believeth in me, though he were dead, yet shall he live.
Jesus wept.
And I, if I be lifted up from the earth, will draw all men unto me.
For they loved the praise of men more than the praise of God.
For I have given you an example, that ye should do as I have done to you.
A new commandment I give unto you, That ye love one another; as I have loved you...
Jesus saith unto him, I am the way, the truth, and the life: no man cometh unto the Father, but by me.
If ye love me, keep my commandments.
Peace I leave with you, my peace I give unto you...
I am the vine, ye are the branches... for without me ye can do nothing.
Greater love hath no man than this, that a man lay down his life for his friends.
Howbeit when he, the Spirit of truth, is come, he will guide you into all truth.
In the world ye shall have tribulation: but be of good cheer; I have overcome the world.
And this is life eternal, that they might know thee the only true God, and Jesus Christ, whom thou hast sent.
That they all may be one; as thou, Father, art in me, and I in thee...
Jesus answered, My kingdom is not of this world.
To this end was I born, and for this cause came I into the world, that I should bear witness unto the truth.
When Jesus therefore saw his mother, and the disciple standing by, whom he loved, he saith unto his mother, Woman, behold thy son!
When Jesus therefore had received the vinegar, he said, It is finished: and he bowed his head, and gave up the ghost.
Then said Jesus to them again, Peace be unto you: as my Father hath sent me, even so send I you.
Jesus saith unto him, Thomas, because thou hast seen me, thou hast believed: blessed are they that have not seen, and yet have believed.
He saith unto him, Feed my lambs.
And there are also many other things which Jesus did... I suppose that even the world itself could not contain the books that should be written.
Beloved... it was needful for me to write unto you, and exhort you that ye should earnestly contend for the faith which was once delivered unto the saints.
And the angels which kept not their first estate, but left their own habitation, he hath reserved in everlasting chains under darkness unto the judgment of the great day.
Now unto him that is able to keep you from falling, and to present you faultless before the presence of his glory with exceeding joy,
For with God nothing shall be impossible.
And thou, child, shalt be called the prophet of the Highest: for thou shalt go before the face of the Lord to prepare his ways;
And the angel said unto them, Fear not: for, behold, I bring you good tidings of great joy, which shall be to all people.
And he said unto them, How is it that ye sought me? wist ye not that I must be about my Father's business?
And Jesus increased in wisdom and stature, and in favour with God and man.
John answered, saying unto them all, I indeed baptize you with water; but one mightier than I cometh... he shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost and with fire:
And Jesus answered him, saying, It is written, That man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word of God.
The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because he hath anointed me to preach the gospel to the poor;
